page: Amazon Web Services c6gn.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs utilizing the AWS Neoverse-N1 model running at 2.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 72,814.16 and a strong Redis SET operations throughput of 58.46 million operations per second. Single-core performance is more moderate, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 974.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1,519.29. Memory bandwidth is stable, reaching 2,839.42 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 167.25 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Large-model LLM inference represents a performance bottleneck, with the 70B model prompt processing and text generation scoring in the poor and weak categories respectively.

The c6gn.12xlarge features a high baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps, making it highly optimized for network-intensive operations. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the custom ARM-based architecture provides a high density of dedicated physical cores and high network throughput relative to its resource profile, offering a favorable alternative to traditional x86 architectures for parallel workloads. This instance is best suited for multi-threaded applications, network-bound workloads, high-performance computing, static web serving, and distributed data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.12xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz. It features 96.0 GB of system memory, a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, highlighted by a stress-ng multi-core score of 72,814.16 and 58.46 million operations per second in Redis SET tests, though single-core metrics and large-model LLM inference are relatively weaker. This hardware profile delivers high network and compute density, making it a cost-efficient choice for multi-threaded, network-intensive workloads. It is highly suited for distributed applications, static web serving, and parallelized compute tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.12xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 48 Graviton cores, 96 GB RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for parallel and network-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.12xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 Graviton cores, 96 GB RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized platform featuring forty-eight dedicated cores and high-speed seventy-five gigabit network bandwidth for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores (arm64)
96.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for high throughput
Strong multi-core performance and high Redis transaction throughput
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for multi-threaded, network-bound, and parallel computing workloads
